threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's quirky to ask for soy milk at the café instead of just asking for milk like everyone else.
Heard a lad bragging about dodging bus fare while a busker sang ‘Ain't No Sunshine’—perfect melancholic backdrop for his story.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I strolled down the Weserstraße and heard a punk band jamming outside a bakery, while the smell of fresh bread mixed with street art vibes. That's when it hit.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Prenzlauer Berg can you find a street fruit vendor next to a craft beer bar, both packed at 2 PM on a Tuesday. Classic Berlin contrast.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to dodge the cast-off burrito wrappers while strolling down Valencia.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy playing mariachi on the 14, and the whole bus started clapping along. Only in the Mission do strangers feel like family.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the plazas; they miss the hidden alleys where locals trade gossip over morning café con leche.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their hesitation at crosswalks—waiting for a green light even when cars are a distant memory.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stand on the platform waiting for the Tube to magically arrive instead of checking the real-time updates on your phone.
Caught a glimpse of a rat casually riding the backseat of the 35 last Saturday. It was totally unfazed by the chaos around it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was really a Neuköllner? Watching a group of kids build a makeshift skate ramp in the playground with just a couple of crates and a plank.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a 24-hour tram that becomes a moving party on weekends, with spontaneous DJ sets and late-night snacks.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local if they can name the taco truck that replaced the old pupusas joint on 24th. It's been a hot topic since last fall.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni stuck behind a street performer doing a fire-breathing act at 6th & Market. Driver just sighed and turned up the radio.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists crowd the benches at Plaça del Sol, oblivious to the locals’ secret spots just a few steps away. They miss out on the best shadows and stories.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they still use Google Maps for every street—look up and you'll see locals navigating by memory.
